subtitle: How Rajaraja Carved His Empire into Stone

panels.shot: majestic low-angle wide of the towering completed temple with Rajaraja in the foreground
panels.action: Rajaraja Chola I stands in royal armor and cloth beside the soaring stone vimana of the Rajarajesvara temple at Thanjavur, looking up with steady pride as the river plain stretches beyond.

dialogue.text: In Thanjavur, in the age of the Imperial Cholas, Rajaraja raised Rajarajesvara, a mountain of stone, music and order.

i18n.hi: थंजावूर में, चोल सम्राटों के युग में, राजराजा ने रजराजेश्वर उठाया – पत्थर, संगीत और अनुशासन का पर्वत।

panels.shot: wide shot of palace terrace overlooking Thanjavur plains at dawn
panels.action: On a palace terrace above the Kaveri plains, Rajaraja Chola I leans on a stone balustrade at dawn, watching the mist over fields and distant village shrines.

dialogue.text: By the turn of the eleventh century, Rajaraja Chola ruled from Thanjavur over Kaveri’s rich plains, ports, and distant coasts.

i18n.hi: ग्यारहवीं सदी की घड़ी बदलते ही, राजराजा चोल थंजावूर से कावेरी की उपजाऊ धरती, बंदरगाहों और दूर के तटों पर राज करता था।

panels.shot: medium close-up on Rajaraja in council hall, maps and palm-leaf documents spread before him
panels.action: Inside a timber-pillared audience hall, Rajaraja sits cross-legged on a low throne, studying palm-leaf records and a painted board of his realm as ministers and scribes attend respectfully in the background.

dialogue.text: Victories in war had filled his treasury and fame, but Rajaraja sought a work that would bind his name to dharma and stone.

i18n.hi: युद्ध की जीतों ने खज़ाना और शोहरत भरे, पर राजराजा ऐसा काम चाहता था जो उसका नाम धर्म और पत्थर से हमेशा बाँध दे।

panels.shot: slow push-in on Rajaraja’s face as he listens to temple musicians in a small shrine
panels.action: Rajaraja kneels barefoot in a modest stone shrine, eyes closed, while a pair of temple musicians play veena and drum before a linga lit by oil lamps.

dialogue.text: In village shrines and ancient temples, he saw the rhythm of worship, the needs of priests, lamps, dancers and drums.

i18n.hi: गाँव के देवस्थानों और प्राचीन मंदिरों में उसने पूजा की लय देखी – पुजारियों, दीपों, नर्तकियों और नगाड़ों की ज़रूरतें।

panels.shot: dramatic mid-shot, low angle, Rajaraja addressing assembled architects and priests in a courtyard
panels.action: In a sunlit palace courtyard, Rajaraja stands tall, gesturing toward a blank stretch of land, as royal architects, sthapatis, priests and accountants sit before him with palm leaves and measuring ropes.

dialogue.text: He declared in Thanjavur a vision: a great Rajarajesvara, where royal power would become a steady service to Shiva.

i18n.hi: उसने थंजावूर में एक स्वप्न सुनाया: विशाल रजराजेश्वर, जहाँ राजसत्ता शिव की नित सेवा बन जाए।

panels.shot: overhead view of the temple plan being traced on packed earth
panels.action: On packed earth, a master architect traces a vast square plan with a stylus and rope while Rajaraja watches, kneeling, as assistants hold measuring cords and small stone models.

dialogue.text: Sthapatis measured and marked the earth. The plan rose from sacred rule-books, experience, and the king’s demand for a soaring vimana.

i18n.hi: स्थापतियों ने धरती नापी, रेखाएँ खींचीं। योजना निकली शास्त्रों, तजुर्बे और राजा की ऊँचे विमान की ज़िद से।

panels.shot: epic wide shot of construction site with scaffolding, elephants, and workers
panels.action: The half-built temple rises with stone tiers and bamboo scaffolds, as workers haul blocks, elephants steady loads, and Rajaraja rides by on horseback inspecting the growing mountain of stone.

dialogue.text: Year by year from about 1003, solid granite blocks climbed on human shoulders, carts and elephants, each one fitted without haste or waste.

i18n.hi: साल दर साल, लगभग १००३ से, ठोस ग्रेनाइट के गट्टे कंधों, बैलगाड़ियों और हाथियों पर चढ़े, हर पत्थर बिना हड़बड़ी, बिना फ़ज़ूल फिट हुआ।

panels.shot: medium shot of Rajaraja speaking quietly with a chief architect beside a carved stone
panels.action: Near a carved pilaster, Rajaraja stands with a chief architect, both examining a sculpted figure while chisels rest nearby and apprentices wait in respectful silence.

dialogue.speaker: rajaraja_chola_i
dialogue.text: Let each stone carry order. No line without purpose.

i18n.hi: हर पत्थर में व्यवस्था बसाओ। कोई रेखा बेकार नहीं।

panels.shot: intimate interior shot of scribe carving on temple wall with Rajaraja dictating
panels.action: Inside a finished corridor, a stonecutter carefully chisels letters into a smooth granite wall while a temple scribe reads from a palm leaf and Rajaraja sits nearby, dictating calmly.

dialogue.text: On those walls, Rajaraja ordered his own voice carved, every gift, every duty recorded in stone.

i18n.hi: उन्हीं दीवारों पर राजराजा ने अपना ही स्वर खुदवाया – हर दान, हर फ़र्ज़ पत्थर में दर्ज।

panels.shot: close-up of inscription panel with Rajaraja’s figure faintly indicated, Rajaraja’s hand pointing
panels.action: A close view shows neat Tamil Grantha letters filling a panel, Rajaraja’s hand entering the frame to point at a line as a young accountant watches, counting on his fingers.

dialogue.text: The inscriptions named villages and fields granted, the measures of paddy and gold, the days and fines if duties failed.

i18n.hi: शिलालेखों ने बख्शी हुई बस्तियों-खेतों के नाम गिनाए, धान और सोने की मात्राएँ, दिन और जुर्माने तय किए अगर फ़र्ज़ चूके।

panels.shot: wide interior shot of temple hall with rows of women dancers and musicians standing in order
panels.action: In a tall columned hall, rows of women dancers and men with veena, drums and conches stand in disciplined lines while Rajaraja sits on a low dais, observing with a composed, judging gaze.

dialogue.text: He endowed wages for hundreds of women attached to the temple, singers, drummers, conch-blowers, and dancing girls to serve in ritual.

i18n.hi: उसने सैकड़ों औरतों की मज़दूरी बाँधी – गायिकाएँ, नगाड़ची, शंखनादक और देवदासियाँ, जो अनुष्ठान में सेवा दें।

panels.shot: medium shot of Rajaraja instructing officials in a treasury room with measured lamps and vessels
panels.action: In a storeroom lined with bronze lamps and vessels, Rajaraja addresses temple stewards and clerks, pointing to neatly stacked oil jars and a board showing tally marks.

dialogue.text: He fixed oil for lamps, cloth for festivals, and timetables for rituals. The temple’s daily life was planned to the measure of a drop of ghee.

i18n.hi: उसने दीपों के तेल, त्योहारों के कपड़े, और पूजा की घड़ियाँ तय कीं। मंदिर की रोज़ की ज़िंदगी घी की बूँद तक नाप कर बँधी।

panels.shot: close-up of an inscription listing temple functionaries while Rajaraja walks past in soft focus
panels.action: Granite walls filled with rows of names of temple functionaries dominate the foreground, while in the background Rajaraja passes slowly with a small entourage, a faint smile on his face.

dialogue.text: The stone lists temple dancers, cooks, watchmen, gardeners, goldsmiths, accountants and supervisors, the unseen hands that kept the deity’s house alive.

i18n.hi: पत्थर की सूचियाँ देवदासियाँ, रसोइए, चौकीदार, माली, सोनार, मुनीम और नज़र रखने वालों के नाम गिनाती हैं – वे अनदेखे हाथ जो देव के घर को ज़िंदा रखते थे।

panels.shot: solemn interior shot of sanctum entrance, Rajaraja barefoot, holding a small casket
panels.action: At the dark threshold of the sanctum, Rajaraja stands barefoot with folded hands, holding a small jewel-studded casket as priests prepare inside with lamps and sacred vessels.

dialogue.text: In 1010, as the temple neared completion, he dedicated jewels and gold for the god’s image, binding royal wealth to daily worship.

i18n.hi: १०१० में, जब मंदिर लगभग पूरा हुआ, उसने देव-प्रतिमा के लिए रत्न और सोना समर्पित किया – राजकोष को रोज़ की पूजा से बाँध दिया।

panels.shot: epic night-time wide shot of kumbhabhisheka ceremony with crowds and flaring torches
panels.action: Under a starry night, scaffolds crown the temple tower as priests pour sanctified water from pots, torches flare, and a vast crowd fills the courtyard while Rajaraja stands below, hands raised in reverence.

dialogue.text: When consecration came, water from sacred rivers bathed the towering finial. Chants, conches and bells filled Thanjavur’s night.

i18n.hi: जब महाकुंभाभिषेक आया, पवित्र नदियों का जल ऊँचे कलश पर बरसा। मंत्र, शंख और घंटियाँ थंजावूर की रात भर गूँज उठीं।

panels.shot: medium shot of Rajaraja and his young heir observing the temple together at sunrise
panels.action: At sunrise, Rajaraja stands beside his young son, both silhouetted against the glowing temple tower, the older king’s hand resting lightly on the boy’s shoulder.

dialogue.speaker: rajaraja_chola_i
dialogue.text: Remember: power fades. Work like this must stand when we are dust.

i18n.hi: याद रखो, सत्ता ढल जाती है। ऐसा काम ही रहता है, जब हम मिट्टी हो जाएँ।

panels.shot: long temporal dissolve from Chola times to present day temple view
panels.action: The scene blends from ancient Chola worshippers in traditional dress circling the temple to modern visitors in varied clothing walking the same stone courtyard beneath the unchanged granite tower.

dialogue.text: A thousand years have passed. Yet Rajarajesvara, now called Brihadisvara, still stands, its walls still speaking the king’s ordered gift of stone, skill and service.

i18n.hi: हज़ार साल बीत गए। पर रजराजेश्वर, अब बृहदिश्वर, अब भी खड़ा है – उसकी दीवारें अब भी राजा की सजी-सँवरी धरोहर: पत्थर, कौशल और सेवा की कहानी कहती हैं।

subtitle_i18n.en: How Rajaraja Carved His Empire into Stone
subtitle_i18n.hi: कैसे राजराजा ने अपना साम्राज्य पत्थर में उकेरा
